LAW AND MOTION 301, THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS --DEFENDANT PACIFIC COAST BANKERS' BANCSHARES, A CALIFORNIA CORPRATION'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 1. MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Peter Bertrand, Esq. of Buchalter & Nemar, 415-227-0900 for defendant and moving party Pacific Coast Bankers' Bancshares
Robert Stumpf. Jr. Esq. of Sheppard Mullin, 415-774-3288 for plaintiff and opposing party First-Citizens Bank & Trust Company

RULING:
ARGUED and the Court modified its tentative ruling as follows --
DEFENDANT PACIFIC COAST BANKERS' BANCSHARES, A CALIFORNIA CORPRATION'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 1. OVERRULED AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 2 BECAUSE PLAINTIFF IS ENTITLED TO PURSUE A DETERMINATION THAT IT COULD NOT BE TERMINATED. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED. Defendant to submit form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 (301/PJB)
